text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. During a review, team members carefully examine a colleague's code before it is merged into the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are much easier and cheaper to fix. Instead of discovering problems later in testing or in production, the team can address them immediately. Additionally, code reviews promote valuable knowledge sharing. Junior developers learn from more experienced peers, and everyone gains exposure to different parts of the codebase. By discussing alternatives and best practices, the whole team improves its coding skills and maintains a high standard of quality. Ultimately, regular code reviews make the software stronger and the team more collaborative.
